title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text: Verify that V⊥is a subspace of Rn.

title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text:
Suppose that V = span(v1, v2, . . . , vk) for some v1, v2, . . . , vk ∈Rn. Express V and V⊥
as subspaces induced by the matrix A =

title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text: Show that (V⊥)⊥= V.

title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text: Show that dim(V) + dim(V⊥) = n.

title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text: Show that V ⊆W for another subspace W implies W⊥⊆V⊥.

title: Orthogonal Complement of a Subspace
text:
Show that every x ∈Rn can be expressed uniquely as x = v + v⊥, where v ∈V and
v⊥∈V⊥. (Hint: Let v be the projection of x on V.)

First of all, A⊤A is invertible for a full-rank tall matrix A ∈Rn×k (To see this
A⊤Ax = 0 =⇒x⊤A⊤Ax = 0 =⇒Ax = 0 =⇒x = 0). For symmetry, consider
(A(A⊤A)−1A⊤)⊤= (A⊤)⊤((A⊤A)−1)⊤A⊤= A((A⊤A)−1)⊤A⊤= A((A⊤A)⊤)−1A⊤=
A(A⊤A)−1A⊤.
Also, consider
(A(A⊤A)−1A⊤)2 = A(A⊤A)−1A⊤A(A⊤A)−1A⊤= A(A⊤A)−1A⊤.

It suffices to show that ∥x −v∥is minimized over all v ∈R(P) by v∗= Px. For
any v ∈R(P):
∥x −v∥2 = ∥x −Px + Px −v∥2 = ∥x −Px∥2 + ∥Px −v∥2 + 2(x −Px)⊤(Px −v)
= ∥x −Px∥2 + ∥Px −v∥2 + 2(x⊤−x⊤P)(Px −v)
= ∥x −Px∥2 + ∥Px −v∥2 + 2(x⊤Px −x⊤v −x⊤P2x + x⊤Pv)
= ∥x −Px∥2 + ∥Px −v∥2 + 2(x⊤Px −x⊤v −x⊤Px + x⊤v)
= ∥x −Px∥2 + ∥Px −v∥2 ≥∥x −Px∥2.
Where we used P = P2 and Pv = v, ∀v ∈R(P)(v = Px = P2x = Pv ). To
achieve equality in the last inequality, we need ∥Px −v∥= 0 →v = Px. Thus,
arg minv∈R(P) ∥x −v∥= Px.

Consider P = uu⊤. Since P = P⊤and P = P2, P is a projection matrix. Since
R(P) = span(u), using part (d) Px is the projection of x onto span(u). This can
also be directly verified since (u⊤x)u = uu⊤x = Px is the component of x in the
direction for the unit vector u.
Problem 5

An Inequality for Orthonormal Matrices: Suppose that the columns of U ∈
Rn×k are orthonormal (i.e. they are orthogonal and have unit l2 norm). Show that
∥UTx∥2 ≤∥x∥2.

title: Householder Reflections
text: Show that Q is orthogonal.

title: Householder Reflections
text:
Show that Qu = −u and that Qv = v for every v ⊥u. Thus, the linear transforma-
tion y = Qx reflects x through the hyperplane with normal vector u.

title: Householder Reflections
text: Given y, find x such that y = Qx.

title: Householder Reflections
text:
Given nonzero vectors x and y, find a unit vector u such that
(I −2uuT)x ∈span(y),
in terms of x and y.

title: Projection Matrices
text: Show that if P is a projection matrix, then so is I −P.

title: Projection Matrices
text:
Suppose that the columns of U ∈Rn×k are orthonormal. Show that UUT is a projec-
tion matrix.

title: Projection Matrices
text:
Suppose that A ∈Rn×k is full-rank with k ≤n. Show that A(ATA)−1AT is a projec-
tion matrix.

title: Projection Matrices
text:
Recall from lectures that the point y ∈S ⊆Rn closest to x ∈Rn is said to be the
orthogonal projection (or projection in short) of x onto S. Show that if P is a projection
matrix, then y = Px is the projection of x onto R(P).

title: Projection Matrices
text:
Let u be a unit vector. Find the projection matrix P such that y = Px is the projection
of x onto span(u).

title: Inner Product for Polynomials
text: Show that g(·, ·) represents an inner product for F4.

title: Inner Product for Polynomials
text: Find an orthogonal basis for F4 using Gram-Schmidt Orthogonalization.
